title: exp_073 -- Entropy Annealing (Late Mean Convergence)
hypothesis: The mean doesn't converge because entropy stays constant. Annealing ent_coef from 0.01 to 0.001 over the last 30% of training forces the distribution to collapse onto the mean, making the mean carry all performance.
what_to_change: From exp_069 baseline, add ent_coef annealing schedule: 0.01 for first 70%, linearly anneal to 0.001 over remaining 30%.
expected_outcome: Deterministic benchmark should improve as mean sharpens late in training.
scope_note: exp_064 failed with ent_coef=0.03 start (3x too high). This starts from the working 0.01 and only anneals late.
